symbols.name: n
symbols.def: Sample size.

symbols.name: d
symbols.def: Dimension of the covariate vector X.

symbols.name: O
symbols.space: {(Y,A,X): Y in R, A in [0,1], X in [0,1]^d}
symbols.def: Observed data unit O = (Y, A, X).

symbols.name: O_1, ..., O_n
symbols.def: Observed iid sample of size n.

symbols.name: Y
symbols.def: Observed outcome.

symbols.name: A
symbols.def: Observed continuous treatment.

symbols.name: X
symbols.def: Observed covariate vector.

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: Observed-data law of O.

symbols.name: P_X
symbols.def: Marginal law of X induced by P.

symbols.name: Y(a)
symbols.def: Potential outcome under treatment level a.

symbols.name: t_0
symbols.def: Interior treatment level at which the dose-response is evaluated.

symbols.name: epsilon_0
symbols.def: Radius of the interior neighborhood around t_0 on which positivity and treatment-direction smoothness are imposed.

symbols.name: alpha
symbols.def: Holder smoothness order of a -> mu_P(a,x) on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

symbols.name: beta
symbols.def: Holder smoothness order of a -> pi_P(a|x) on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

symbols.name: s
symbols.def: Common Holder smoothness order in the covariate direction for x -> mu_P(t_0,x), x -> pi_P(t_0|x), and the density of P_X.

symbols.name: M
symbols.def: Generic envelope constant for boundedness and Holder radii.

symbols.name: c_0
symbols.def: Lower positivity bound in the neighborhood of t_0.

symbols.name: mu_P
symbols.def: mu_P(a,x) = E_P[Y | A = a, X = x].

symbols.name: pi_P
symbols.def: pi_P(a|x) is the conditional density of A given X = x under P.

symbols.name: p_{X,P}
symbols.def: Density of P_X with respect to Lebesgue measure.

symbols.name: theta_P(t_0)
symbols.def: theta_P(t_0) = E_P[mu_P(t_0,X)] = E_P[Y(t_0)] under the causal assumptions.

symbols.name: R_n
symbols.def: Pointwise minimax mean-squared risk for estimating theta_P(t_0).

symbols.name: rho_n
symbols.def: Published Bonvini-Kennedy benchmark rate sequence; in the smooth-covariate regime it collapses to the oracle exponent n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)}.

assumptions.condition: O_1, ..., O_n are iid draws from P.
Y = Y(A) almost surely under P.
For every a in [0,1], Y(a) is independent of A conditional on X under P.
[t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] is contained in (0,1).
pi_P(a|x) >= c_0 for every a in [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] and every x in [0,1]^d.
For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> mu_P(a,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order alpha and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the treatment coordinate

assumptions.condition: For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> pi_P(a|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order beta and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the treatment coordinate

assumptions.condition: The function x -> mu_P(t_0,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the covariate coordinate

assumptions.condition: The function x -> pi_P(t_0|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the covariate coordinate

assumptions.condition: The density p_{X,P} belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d and is bounded above by M.

There exist a density p_0 on [0,1]^d, a conditional treatment density q_0 on [0,1], and a constant eta_0>0 such that p_0 and q_0 have Holder norms at most M-eta_0 in the smoothness classes required of p_{X,P} and pi_P, q_0(a) >= c_0+eta_0 for all a in [t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], and p_0 is bounded above by M-eta_0.

novel.justification: standard: nonempty interior-of-model condition used in minimax lower bounds for Holder balls and overlap classes; cf. Tsybakov (2009, Nonparametric Estimation, lower-bound constructions) and Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman (2022, minimax causal lower bounds).

definitions.name: P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0)
definitions.construction: {P : P satisfies ass:iid-sampling, ass:consistency, ass:no-unmeasured-confounding, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:interior-dose, ass:local-positivity, ass:mu-treatment-holder, ass:pi-treatment-holder, ass:mu-covariate-holder, ass:pi-covariate-holder, ass:px-holder}

definitions.name: R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0)
definitions.construction: R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) = inf_{hat_theta_n} sup_{P in P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0)} E_P[(hat_theta_n - theta_P(t_0))^2]

definitions.name: beta-frontier handle
definitions.construction: Headline and frontier handle. The delivered content is the all-beta lower floor: by a de-laundered two-point Le Cam construction perturbing only mu_P with the covariate density and treatment-density baselines fixed, the original class satisfies R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} for every beta>0. When s>=d/4, lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra identifies the published benchmark rho_n with this same exponent, so the converse lands on the classical interior treatment-regression scale. When 0<s<d/4, rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4*s)+1/alpha)} is recorded only as the published HOIF comparator, not as a same-class certified upper endpoint. The residual carried by oeq:full-beta-frontier is therefore the unrestricted upper frontier itself: can one attain rho_n on the original class, or does a genuinely beta-sensitive phase intervene?

statements.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. Then for every beta>0 there exists a constant c > 0, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack baseline, such that for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)). Equivalently, uniformly over every beta>0, every estimator of theta_P(t_0) over P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0) has worst-case mean-squared error at least a constant multiple of the classical interior treatment-regression pointwise rate.
statements.justification: This is the certified kernel: a two-point converse that survives for every beta>0 because the least-favorable construction freezes the treatment density and perturbs only the treatment regression.
statements.gap: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF gives the fastest published upper rate but explicitly states that a tight lower bound remains elusive; KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE supplies the closest fuzzy-hypothesis template, but not the continuous-dose localization with density normalization.
statements.consumer: It supplies the universal lower floor that every later frontier claim must respect and remains the durable benchmark even where same-class upper-side sharpness is still open.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Fix beta>0 and write C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). The embedded two-point subexperiment constructed in lem:oracle-dose-regression-lower-all-beta is contained in C_{alpha,beta,s}: it uses the strict-interior baseline p_0 and q_0 from ass:baseline-submodel-slack, keeps the covariate density and treatment density fixed, and perturbs only the treatment regression by the local Holder-alpha bump mu_zeta(a,x)=zeta lambda h_n^alpha psi((a-t_0)/h_n). The support condition ass:interior-dose keeps this perturbation inside the local treatment window, ass:local-positivity and the baseline slack keep the fixed treatment density admissible, ass:px-holder and the pi Holder assumptions are inherited from p_0 and q_0, and the Bernoulli mean channel on {-B,B} gives |Y|<=M while realizing the desired outcome regression. The causal overlay in the lemma gives consistency and no unmeasured confounding, so the causal target is the identifying functional theta_P(t_0)=int mu_P(t_0,x)p_{X,P}(x)dx from def:theta-functional. For h_n=a_0 n^{-1/(2 alpha+1)}, the lemma proves that the two n-sample laws have KL bounded by a fixed finite constant and that their target separation is Delta_n=2 lambda a_0^alpha n^{-alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Applying the declared Le Cam two-point lemma inside this subexperiment gives, for every estimator T_n, max_{zeta in {-1,1}} E_{P_zeta}[(T_n-theta_{P_zeta}(t_0))^2] >= c_K Delta_n^2 >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Since the two laws P_+ and P_- are members of C_{alpha,beta,s}, the supremum risk over C_{alpha,beta,s} is at least this two-point maximum. Taking the infimum over all estimators in def:minimax-risk yields R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for all sufficiently large n. The constants depend only on the fixed radii, the chosen universal bump, and the slack baseline, not on n. Because q_0 is not perturbed, no step uses beta>=alpha; the same lower bound holds for every beta>0.

statements.statement: Under the assumptions of thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, if s >= d/4 then rho_n = n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) and therefore R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) = c rho_n for some c > 0. Thus the certified lower floor reduces to the classical interior pointwise nonparametric regression barrier.
statements.justification: A sharp converse needs a transparent sanity reduction showing that once the covariate nuisances are smooth enough, no causal complication beats the ordinary local-regression difficulty.
statements.gap: The reduction is implicit in BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF and classical regression theory, but the interior dose-response literature does not currently state it as part of a unified minimax frontier.
statements.consumer: It gives the referee-visible special case that collapses to a known result and clarifies exactly when the nuisance-product obstruction disappears.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Work under the assumptions of thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound and fix beta>0. Put C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). By thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, applied to this same class and to the minimax risk functional in def:minimax-risk, there are constants c>0 and n_0<infinity, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack baseline, such that for every n>=n_0, R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. It remains only to check the algebraic collapse of the published rate in def:published-hoif-rate. Write a=2 alpha/(2 alpha+1) and b=2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha), so rho_n=n^{-a} vee n^{-b}. If s>=d/4, then d/(4s)<=1. Hence 1+d/(4s)+1/alpha <= 2+1/alpha=(2 alpha+1)/alpha, and therefore b=2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) >= 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)=a. For every n>=1, b>=a implies n^{-b}<=n^{-a}; consequently rho_n=n^{-a}=n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Combining this identity with the preceding minimax lower bound gives R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} = c rho_n whenever s>=d/4 and n is sufficiently large. Thus, in the smooth-covariate regime, the certified lower floor is exactly the classical interior pointwise nonparametric treatment-regression barrier.

statements.statement: Under baseline-submodel slack, for every fixed beta > 0 the original dose-response class has minimax MSE at least c n^(-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)) for all sufficiently large n. This lower floor matches the published rho_n exponent when s >= d/4; when 0 < s < d/4, rho_n has a strictly smaller exponent and remains only an upper-side comparator, so no same-class upper endpoint is asserted.
statements.justification: Bonvini-Kennedy themselves note that the treatment-density smoothness beta may enter their upper bound only through proof bookkeeping, so the full beta-frontier is the real unresolved mathematical content beyond the clean subregime.
statements.gap: No published paper determines whether the beta dependence in continuous-dose HOIF rates is structural or an artifact of the current upper-bound argument.
statements.consumer: Resolving this question would complete the anisotropic phase diagram and determine whether future adaptive confidence procedures must learn beta as a genuine hardness parameter.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Let C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), and let R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) be the minimax MSE for the causal dose-response value theta_P(t_0)=int_{[0,1]^d} mu_P(t_0,x)p_{X,P}(x) dx. The identifying equality with E_P[Y(t_0)] is the given consistency and ignorability identification statement; the present argument concerns estimation risk for this already identified functional. The quantitative certificate is lem:certified-beta-frontier-bracket. That lemma supplies the universal lower floor c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every fixed beta>0. If s>=d/4, lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra identifies rho_n with n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}, so the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark. If 0<s<d/4, lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra identifies rho_n=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and proves that its exponent is strictly smaller than 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Thus the unresolved content is now exactly what def:beta-frontier-handle says: whether the original class admits a same-class upper bound of order rho_n, an intermediate exponent, or a genuinely beta-sensitive phase. No proof below asserts a certified same-class upper endpoint.

statements.statement: Fix B>0. Let Q_u and Q_v be distributions on {-B,B} with means u and v, respectively, so Q_u(B)=(1+u/B)/2 and Q_v(B)=(1+v/B)/2. If |u|<=B/2 and |v|<=B/2, then KL(Q_u,Q_v) <= 2*(u-v)^2/B^2.
statements.proof_tex: Put p=(1+u/B)/2 and q=(1+v/B)/2. The restrictions |u|,|v|<=B/2 imply p,q in [1/4,3/4]. The Bernoulli KL divergence satisfies KL(Ber(p),Ber(q)) <= (p-q)^2/[q(1-q)], because log x <= x-1 gives p log(p/q)+(1-p)log((1-p)/(1-q)) <= (p-q)^2/[q(1-q)]. Since q(1-q)>=3/16 and p-q=(u-v)/(2B), we get KL(Q_u,Q_v) <= (16/3)*(u-v)^2/(4B^2) <= 2*(u-v)^2/B^2.

statements.statement: Let Q_0 and Q_1 be two laws for the n-sample experiment and let theta_0,theta_1 be the corresponding values of a real parameter, with Delta=|theta_1-theta_0|. If KL(Q_0,Q_1)<=K<infinity, then every estimator T satisfies max_{i=0,1} E_{Q_i}[(T-theta_i)^2] >= c_K Delta^2 for a constant c_K>0 depending only on K.
statements.proof_tex: Source lemma. This is the standard two-point reduction from estimation to testing; see Tsybakov, Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation, 2009, Chapter 2, especially Theorem 2.2 and the preceding testing reduction. In the form used here, for any estimator T, the event that T is closer to the wrong parameter induces a test between Q_0 and Q_1, giving max_i E_{Q_i}[(T-theta_i)^2] >= (Delta^2/16) inf_phi {Q_0(phi=1)+Q_1(phi=0)}. The Bretagnolle-Huber/Le Cam inequality bounds the testing error below by a positive constant depending only on KL(Q_0,Q_1) when KL(Q_0,Q_1)<=K. Combining these inequalities yields the stated c_K Delta^2 bound.

statements.statement: If s >= d/4, then the published beta>=alpha rate rho_n satisfies rho_n = n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every n >= 1.
statements.proof_tex: Let e_1=2 alpha/(2 alpha+1) and e_2=2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha). The definition def:published-hoif-rate says rho_n=n^{-e_1} vee n^{-e_2}. If s>=d/4, then d/(4s)<=1. Hence 1+d/(4s)+1/alpha <= 2+1/alpha=(2 alpha+1)/alpha. Taking reciprocals of positive quantities gives e_2 >= alpha*2/(2 alpha+1)=e_1. For n>=1, the map e -> n^{-e} is nonincreasing, so n^{-e_2} <= n^{-e_1}. Therefore the maximum in def:published-hoif-rate is n^{-e_1}, i.e. rho_n=n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}.

statements.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every beta>0 there is a constant c_or>0, depending only on the fixed model radii and on the slack baseline but not on n, such that for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c_or n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. The construction leaves the treatment density fixed, so the bound is independent of whether beta>=alpha.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Fix beta>0 and write C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). Let I=[t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], which is contained in (0,1) by ass:interior-dose. By ass:baseline-submodel-slack choose a covariate density p_0, a conditional treatment density q_0, and eta_0>0 satisfying the stated Holder, boundedness, and positivity requirements with strict slack. Choose B=M/2. Let psi be a nonzero C-infinity bump supported on (-1,1) with psi(0)=1. Its Holder-alpha norm on R is finite. For h>0 and zeta in {-1,1}, define mu_zeta(a,x)=zeta lambda h^alpha psi((a-t_0)/h), where lambda>0 is fixed. For all sufficiently small h, [t_0-h,t_0+h] is contained in I. The scaled bump has treatment-coordinate Holder-alpha norm bounded by a constant times lambda, uniformly in h and x. At a=t_0, the map x -> mu_zeta(t_0,x)=zeta lambda h^alpha is constant, so its covariate Holder-s seminorm is zero and its supremum is lambda h^alpha. Taking lambda small enough, and then h small enough, gives ass:mu-treatment-holder and ass:mu-covariate-holder. Define P_zeta by X having density p_0, A|X having density q_0, and Y|(A=a,X=x) following the two-point mean channel on {-B,B} with P_zeta(Y=B|A=a,X=x)=(1+mu_zeta(a,x)/B)/2. Since |mu_zeta(a,x)|<=lambda h^alpha ||psi||_infty, for all sufficiently large n this probability is in [1/4,3/4], and |Y|<=B<=M, so ass:bounded-outcome holds. A causal realization is obtained by drawing U uniformly on [0,1], independently of A conditional on X, and setting Y_zeta(a)=B*(2*1{U <= (1+mu_zeta(a,X)/B)/2}-1). Then Y=Y_zeta(A), and the potential-outcome process is independent of A conditional on X. Thus ass:consistency and ass:no-unmeasured-confounding hold. Because p_0 and q_0 are unchanged across zeta and satisfy the slack baseline, ass:px-holder, ass:local-positivity, ass:pi-treatment-holder, and ass:pi-covariate-holder hold. The beta condition is automatic because q_0 is fixed and admissible for the declared beta. Hence P_+ and P_- belong to C_{alpha,beta,s}. By def:theta-functional, theta_{P_zeta}(t_0)=int mu_zeta(t_0,x)p_0(x)dx=zeta lambda h^alpha, because psi(0)=1 and p_0 integrates to one. Therefore Delta_h=|theta_{P_+}(t_0)-theta_{P_-}(t_0)|=2 lambda h^alpha. The one-observation KL has no contribution from X or A|X because those laws are common. Conditional on (A,X), lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl gives KL(Q_{mu_+(A,X)},Q_{mu_-(A,X)}) <= 2*(mu_+(A,X)-mu_-(A,X))^2/B^2. The difference equals 2 lambda h^alpha psi((A-t_0)/h). Since q_0 is bounded above by a constant supplied by its Holder radius, KL(P_+,P_-) <= C lambda^2 h^{2 alpha} int psi((a-t_0)/h)^2 q_0(a) da <= C' lambda^2 h^{2 alpha+1}. Under ass:iid-sampling, KL(P_+^n,P_-^n)=n KL(P_+,P_-). Set h_n=a_0 n^{-1/(2 alpha+1)} with a_0>0 small enough that h_n<=epsilon_0 for all large n and n KL(P_+,P_-)<=K<infinity. Lemma lem:le-cam-two-point-mse-source then implies that every estimator T_n satisfies max_{zeta in {-1,1}} E_{P_zeta}[(T_n-theta_{P_zeta}(t_0))^2] >= c_K Delta_{h_n}^2. Since Delta_{h_n}^2=4 lambda^2 a_0^{2 alpha} n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}, the worst-case risk over C_{alpha,beta,s} is at least a constant multiple of n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Taking the infimum over T_n in def:minimax-risk proves the claim. The constants are fixed by p_0, q_0, eta_0, B, psi, lambda, a_0, and K; hence they may depend on the fixed class radii and the slack baseline, but not on n. Since q_0 is never perturbed, the same argument works for every beta>0.

statements.statement: If 0<s<d/4, then rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} for every n>=1, and 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1).
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Put a=2 alpha/(2 alpha+1) and b=2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha). The definition def:published-hoif-rate says rho_n=n^{-a} vee n^{-b}. If 0<s<d/4, then d/(4s)>1, so 1+d/(4s)+1/alpha > 2+1/alpha=(2 alpha+1)/alpha. Taking reciprocals of positive quantities gives b < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)=a. For n>=1, b<a implies n^{-b}>=n^{-a}. Therefore rho_n=n^{-b}=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}, and the deficient functional exponent is strictly smaller than the oracle exponent.

statements.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every fixed beta>0 and every s>=d/4, there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} = c rho_n. Thus, in the smooth-covariate regime, the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark rho_n, but this core does not claim a matching same-class upper bound.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Fix beta>0 and s>=d/4, and abbreviate C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). By thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, under ass:baseline-submodel-slack there is c_l>0 such that R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c_l n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for all sufficiently large n. Lemma lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra gives rho_n=n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} when s>=d/4. Substituting this identity yields R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c_l rho_n. This is a same-scale lower-floor comparison only; the present DAG does not derive a matching same-class upper inequality.
statements.justification: Once the converse is proved, the clean regime s>=d/4 is the referee-visible sanity check: the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark without laundering in an unresolved upper route.
statements.gap: The literature does not separate same-scale comparison from same-class sharpness; making that distinction explicit is necessary once the upper-side route is not reproduced on the bare class.
statements.consumer: It tells the reader exactly what is settled in the smooth-covariate regime: the converse reaches the benchmark exponent, while the matching same-class upper theorem remains external.

statements.statement: For every fixed beta>0 and every 0<s<d/4, under the declared same-class assumptions and ass:baseline-submodel-slack, there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. Moreover rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} has a strictly smaller exponent than the certified lower floor. The same-class upper endpoint C rho_n is not discharged on the present core.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Fix beta>0 and 0<s<d/4, and write C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). By lem:oracle-dose-regression-lower-all-beta, under ass:baseline-submodel-slack there is c_l>0 such that R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c_l n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for all sufficiently large n. That lower construction perturbs only mu_P and keeps p_{X,P} and pi_P fixed at the slack baseline, so it is valid for every s and every beta>0. Lemma lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra identifies rho_n with n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and proves that this exponent is strictly smaller than 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Hence the present core certifies only the lower endpoint together with the algebraic benchmark comparison; it does not derive a same-class upper bound of order rho_n.
statements.justification: The deficient regime is exactly where the temptation to overclaim is highest, so the honest theorem must separate the certified lower floor from the still-external functional-branch comparator.
statements.gap: The literature offers a deficient-regime HOIF benchmark, but the present class does not yet discharge that same-class upper endpoint because the localized upper route needs more than slice smoothness.
statements.consumer: It tells the reader what survives in the deficient regime and what remains to be proved before any two-sided frontier can be advertised.

statements.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. For every fixed beta>0 there exists c>0 such that, for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. If s>=d/4 then rho_n = n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}. If 0<s<d/4 then rho_n = n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and 2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha) < 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Hence the certified unrestricted beta-frontier is the all-beta oracle lower floor together with the algebraic comparison to the published rho_n benchmark.
statements.proof_tex: Proof. Fix beta>0 and abbreviate C_{alpha,beta,s}=P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0). The all-beta lower floor comes directly from thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound: there are constants c>0 and n_0<infinity such that R_n(C_{alpha,beta,s},t_0) >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} for every n>=n_0. If s>=d/4, lem:rho-oracle-regime-algebra identifies rho_n with n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}, so the certified lower floor is of order rho_n. If 0<s<d/4, lem:rho-deficient-regime-algebra identifies rho_n=n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)} and proves that its exponent is strictly smaller than 2 alpha/(2 alpha+1). Therefore the present certificate is exactly the universal lower floor plus the regime-by-regime algebraic comparison to the published benchmark; it does not package any same-class upper theorem.

statements.statement: Cited conditional comparator (Bonvini-Kennedy 2022). Under Assumptions 1-2 and Conditions 1-4 of the source theorem, alpha <= beta, equal covariate smoothness s for the two nuisance functions, projection approximation of order k^(-2s/d), the source's optimal choices of k and h, equal-rate estimation of w and 1/pi, and negligibility of the third-order density-estimation remainder ||v||_g ||q||_g ||p_hat(t,.)-p(t,.)||_infinity, the conditional MSE of the source's specified second-order estimator is eventually at most a constant times rho_n as displayed in Figure 1. This is an estimator-specific conditional risk implication, not an unconditional minimax upper bound over any class and not a same-class upper theorem for def:holder-dose-class.

source.locator: arXiv:2207.11825v1, Section 3.4, Theorem 1 and Remarks 7-8/Figure 1 (renumbered Theorem 3.1 in v2)
source.verbatim_statement: The source bounds the conditional bias and variance of its specified mth-order HOIF estimator under Assumptions 1-2 and Conditions 1-4. Its Figure 1 rho_n comparison specializes to the second-order estimator, alpha <= beta, gamma1 = gamma2 = s, projection approximation k^(-(gamma1+gamma2)/d), source-optimal k and h, equal-rate estimation of w and 1/pi, and negligibility of ||v||_g ||q||_g ||p_hat(t,.)-p(t,.)||_infinity. Under those conditions the estimator's conditional MSE has the displayed rho_n order. The source does not state an unconditional minimax upper theorem over arbitrary covariate-density smoothness sp and gives no numerical sp threshold.

attestation.note: Manually transcribed and checked against the lawful arXiv primary source, v1 Section 3.4 Theorem 1 and Remarks 7-8/Figure 1; current v2 renumbers the result Theorem 3.1.

sampling_model.treatment: A_i is a scalar continuous treatment supported on [0,1]
sampling_model.overlap: The conditional treatment density pi_P(a|x) is bounded below on an epsilon_0-neighborhood of the interior dose t_0
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: No estimator is built into the class; nuisance difficulty is encoded by Holder regularity of mu_P, pi_P, and p_{X,P}

tldr: The note now certifies an all-beta lower floor for interior continuous-treatment dose-response estimation at a fixed dose t_0: over the original Holder class, every estimator of theta_P(t_0) has worst-case MSE at least a constant multiple of n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)). Bonvini and Kennedy remain the upper-side benchmark, but the current core no longer re-derives their HOIF route on the bare class because the localized pilot and score steps need extra window/joint regularity beyond the slice assumptions at t_0. In the smooth-covariate regime s>=d/4 the certified lower floor lands on the same exponent as rho_n; in the deficient regime the same-class upper frontier remains open.

project_justification.gap: The interior fixed-dose dose-response literature has a published higher-order upper rate but no matching minimax converse, so the central frontier is still one-sided.
project_justification.niche: The target theta_P(t_0) is neither ordinary regression nor an ordinary nonlinear functional: the hard subexperiment must localize in the treatment coordinate while preserving conditional-density normalization and positivity.
project_justification.fill: The project fills that gap by adapting the CATE fuzzy-hypothesis architecture to a continuous-dose partial-mean target, proving an all-beta pointwise lower floor on the original class, showing in the smooth-covariate regime that this lower floor lands on the same exponent as the published benchmark rho_n, and isolating the unresolved same-class upper frontier instead of folding extra regularity into the main theorem.

related_work: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF is the anchor because it introduces the continuous-dose HOIF estimator, separates treatment-direction and covariate-direction smoothness, and states that a tight lower bound remains elusive. The present core now treats that paper as the upper-side benchmark rather than re-deriving its localized nuisance route on the bare Holder class, because the published upper analysis uses window/joint regularity beyond the slice assumptions encoded here. KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017ContinuousDR is the first pseudo-outcome dose-response precursor because it establishes the regression-plus-nuisance-remainder representation that later estimators refine. ColangeloLee2020ContinuousDML and SemenovaChernozhukov2017CausalFunctions are the main first-order comparators: both provide orthogonal-score or orthogonal-signal inference machinery for continuous or function-valued causal targets, but neither characterizes the minimax frontier for interior fixed-dose estimation. KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE is the closest lower-bound template because it proves a hybrid regression-functional rate by localized fuzzy hypotheses, yet its construction does not enforce continuous-treatment density normalization or fixed-dose localization. RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2008HOIF and RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016HOIFTechnical provide the higher-order von Mises and U-statistic logic underlying the Bonvini-Kennedy upper analysis that this core records only as an external comparator. SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous is the natural foil because it delivers genuine continuous-exposure lower bounds, but for incremental tilts and edge-dose limits rather than the interior fixed-dose partial mean. BibautVanderLaan2017AdaptiveSmoothing explains why closing even the lower side of the rate frontier matters for inference: fixed-dose dose-response values are nonregular, so any later adaptive confidence procedure must know the benchmark it is adapting to. CinelliEtAl2025CausalChallenges underscores that minimax optimality for modern causal functionals remains a live open problem rather than a settled technicality.
honest_scope: The settled kernel in this version is the all-beta pointwise lower floor R_n >= c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} on the original Holder class. In the smooth-covariate regime s>=d/4 this puts the converse on the same exponent as the published rho_n benchmark, but the note does not certify a matching same-class upper bound. In the deficient regime 0<s<d/4 the Bonvini-Kennedy functional branch remains an external comparator whose same-class validity is open on the present assumptions. The explicit frontier question is whether rho_n is attainable on the original class, whether an intermediate exponent appears, or whether beta becomes a genuine hardness parameter.
